one_liner: Incident replay for distributed systems — rewind any production failure.
why_now: Tracing finally standardised on OpenTelemetry, so the event stream needed to replay an incident now exists in most stacks — it did not two years ago.
prior_companies: Built and sold a load-testing tool to a monitoring vendor in 2022

sanity.note: Live open-source product, named technical founder, revenue-generating design partners.

breakdown.label: Geography
breakdown.note: Netherlands is in the EU focus region

strengths: Technical founder with a prior exit in the same adjacent category (load testing → observability)
Ships publicly — the replay engine is open source with steady commit history and external contributors
Writes credibly about distributed-systems debugging, which is how the first design partners found them
weaknesses: Solo founder — no named GTM or commercial counterpart yet
Prior exit was small and the terms are not public, so the outcome is hard to size
opportunities: A commercial co-founder would remove the single biggest execution risk
Existing open-source contributors are a natural first hiring pool
threats: Solo-founder companies stall disproportionately at the seed-to-A transition

strengths: Observability spend keeps growing while buyers actively look to cut incumbent bills
OpenTelemetry standardisation removed the integration work that blocked this category
Clear budget owner — platform and SRE teams already pay for tooling in this line item
weaknesses: Crowded adjacent market dominated by very well-funded incumbents
Buyers consolidate tools in downturns, which pressures a point solution
opportunities: Incumbent pricing backlash is pushing teams to unbundle their observability stack
threats: An incumbent could ship replay as a feature and bundle it at no extra cost
evidence: Multiple 2025 vendor reports cite observability cost control as a top-three platform priority

strengths: Replay is a genuinely differentiated wedge, not another dashboard
Open-source distribution gives a credible bottom-up path into teams that will not take a sales call
weaknesses: Deck does not show conversion from open-source users to paying customers
Pricing model is unstated, and this category is unusually sensitive to per-volume pricing
opportunities: Land-and-expand from a single team to a platform-wide contract is well-proven here
threats: If replay becomes a checkbox feature, the wedge closes before expansion revenue arrives
evidence: Deck lists 4 design partners but no stated conversion or contract values

findings.summary: Northwind Labs open-sourced its replay engine, which reconstructs a production incident from OpenTelemetry traces so engineers can step through the failure after the fact.
Coverage of the debugging-tools space names Northwind among a group of early companies attacking incident response from the replay angle rather than dashboards.
Founder discussed the limits of trace sampling in a conference talk, arguing most teams discard the data needed to reconstruct the incidents they care about.

signals: Commits across 3 years with no long gaps
Maintains a repo with external contributors, not just personal projects
Reviews and merges other people's pull requests

sample_posts.text: Sampling traces at 1% then asking why you can't debug the outage is the observability equivalent of deleting your logs to save disk.
Replay is now open source. If you can reconstruct the incident, the postmortem writes itself.

findings.summary: Named as founder of Northwind Labs and previously the technical lead on a load-testing tool acquired by a monitoring vendor in 2022.
